findings.description: Root loader in apps/web/app/root.tsx does not load or supply googleIdentityEnabled, googleClientId, and devAuthEnabled to the frontend.
Google login UI remains disabled or hidden because googleIdentityEnabled evaluates to false by default.
Dev bypass relies on sending a fake token from client instead of a dedicated, secure POST /api/v1/dev-auth/login backend route guarded against production environment.
There is no rate limiting on the Google auth endpoint, exposing it to potential brute force or abuse.
Internal database or validation exceptions might be returned with their raw message to the client, risking data leak.
First-time login lacks TransactionManager protection, causing potential orphan Users or unique constraint violations under race conditions.
Concurrent favorite requests can result in database integrity constraint violations instead of idempotent upsert behavior.
Visit PATCH operation clears or updates omitted fields like note to null instead of preserving their original state.
Validation for visitedOn allows invalid formats or dates in the future.
Private endpoints /api/v1/me/** do not return Cache-Control: private, no-store, permitting proxy or client caching.
Personalization place-state endpoint performs N+1 database queries to check individual place favorite/visit status.
Account favorite and visit pages fetch full details per place using individual requests rather than a batch query.
New auth and personalization endpoints are not fully documented in OpenAPI nor generated via api-generate.
There is no complete Playwright integration testing suite for C3 product experience and security flows.
